lesson: The C# repo must own reusable authoring, analysis, preset, scoring, and Faust-generation behavior; only the Rust renderer/runtime and experimental compiler branch are intentionally left behind.
source: user direction and migration pass through commit 3246672

lesson: Runtime-facing audio code must avoid unbounded operations: allocation, locks, I/O, console output, OS calls, and context switches do not belong in audio callbacks.
Treat patch scripts as deterministic dataflow graphs with explicit rate and block semantics; terse syntax is acceptable only when lowering remains inspectable.
source: Pure Data block/subpatch model and Faust block-diagram model

lesson: SFXR normalized filter values are not linear cutoff frequencies. In Faust export, small SFXR high-pass values must remain very low; mapping hpf directly to thousands of Hz removes the body of pickup, jump, powerup, and blip presets while still leaving them audible enough for weak tests to pass.
source: Aquarium engine native Faust parity test against C# SFXR reference renderer

lesson: BFXR, SFXR, 808, FM, and wobble presets are reference patches for testing and DSL design, not terseness trophies. Built-ins should be readable enough to teach the patch language, and the parser should support multi-line commands instead of forcing all expressive patches into one-line golf.
source: user clarified preset purpose for patch DSL iteration
